ElementDescription: The NDAR Global Unique Identifier (GUID) for research subject
Subject ID how it's defined in lab/project
Date on which the interview/genetic test/sampling/imaging/biospecimen was completed. MM/DD/YYYY
Age in months at the time of the interview/test/sampling/imaging.
Notes: Age is rounded to chronological month. If the research participant is 15-days-old at time of interview, the appropriate value would be 0 months. If the participant is 16-days-old, the value would be 1 month.

ElementDescription: Sex of subject at birth
Notes: M = Male; F = Female; O=Other; NR = Not reported

ElementDescription: Date the assessment was completed
PD-Q Scale of pain now
PD-Q Scale of strongest pain in past 4 weeks
PD-Q Scale of average pain strength in past 4 weeks
PD-Q Best description of the participant/subject's course of pain
Notes: 1 = Persistent pain with slight fluctuation; 2 = Persistent pain with pain attacks; 3 = Pain attacks without pain between them; 4 = Pain attacks with pain between them

ElementDescription: PD-Q anatomic site areas where the participant has pain
Notes: (data recorded for each site - see items named PDQPainMapxx)

ElementDescription: Indicator of pain radiation in the Pain Detect Questionnaire (PDQ)
PD-Q areas where the pain radiates
Notes: (data recorded for each site - see items named PDQPainMapxx)

ElementDescription: Scale of extent to which the participant/subject suffers from burning OR stinging sensation in the Pain Detect Questionnaire (PDQ)
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = Hardly notice; 2 = Slightly; 3 = Moderately; 4 = Strongly; 5 = Very strongly

ElementDescription: Scale of extent to which the participant/subject has tingling or electrical sensation in the area of pain, as part of the Pain Detect Questionnaire (PDQ)
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = Hardly notice; 2 = Slightly; 3 = Moderately; 4 = Strongly; 5 = Very strongly

ElementDescription: Scale of extent to which light touch causes pain, in the Pain Detect Questionnaire (PDQ)
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = Hardly notice; 2 = Slightly; 3 = Moderately; 4 = Strongly; 5 = Very strongly

ElementDescription: Scale of extent to which the participant/subject experiences sudden pain attacks like electric shocks in the painful area, as part of the Pain Detect Questionnaire (PDQ)
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = Hardly notice; 2 = Slightly; 3 = Moderately; 4 = Strongly; 5 = Very strongly

ElementDescription: Scale of thermal sensitivity in the Pain Detect Questionnaire (PDQ)
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = Hardly notice; 2 = Slightly; 3 = Moderately; 4 = Strongly; 5 = Very strongly

ElementDescription: Scale of extent to which the participant/subject experiences numbness in the painful area, as part of the Pain Detect Questionnaire (PDQ)
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = Hardly notice; 2 = Slightly; 3 = Moderately; 4 = Strongly; 5 = Very strongly

ElementDescription: Scale of extent to which slight pressure triggers pain, in the Pain Detect Questionnaire (PDQ)
Notes: 0 = Never; 1 = Hardly notice; 2 = Slightly; 3 = Moderately; 4 = Strongly; 5 = Very strongly

ElementDescription: Sum score of PD-Q items 8-14
Notes: Calculated; Sum of PDQburningScl, PDQtinglingScl, PDQpainLgtTouchScl, PDQsuddenAttackPainScl, PDQthermSensitivScl, PDQnumbnessScl, PDQpressureScl

ElementDescription: Recode of pain radiating question PDQpainRadiatYN
Notes: 0= No (pain radiation not indicated); 2= Yes (pain radiation indicated)

ElementDescription: Recode of pain course question PDQpainCourse
Notes: -1= Persistent pain with pain attacks; 0= Persistent pain with slight fluctuation; 1= Pain attacks with or without pain between them

ElementDescription: PDQ summary score

Indicator that the respondent marked area 3, corresponding to the upper arm on the left side

Indicator that the respondent marked area 5, corresponding to the lower arm on the left side
Indicator that the respondent marked area 6, corresponding to the lower arm on the right side
Indicator that the respondent marked area 7, corresponding to the hip (buttock) on the left side
Indicator that the respondent marked area 8, corresponding to the hip (buttock) on the right side
Indicator that the respondent marked area 9,corresponding to the upper leg on the left side
Indicator that the respondent marked area 10, corresponding to the upper leg on the right side
Indicator that the respondent marked area 11, corresponding to the lower leg on the left side
Indicator that the respondent marked area 12, corresponding to the lower leg on the right side
Indicator that the respondent marked area 13, corresponding to the jaw on the left side
Indicator that the respondent marked area 14, corresponding to the jaw on the right side
